name: Wild Duck Gumbo
recipeInstructions:
1. In a Dutch oven over medium heat, brown the duck in batches and remove it, setting aside.
2. Discard all but 2/3 cup of the drippings.
3. Add flour to the drippings; cook and stir over medium heat until brown, about 12-14 minutes.
4. Add the sausage, onion, green pepper, celery, parsley, and garlic. Cook for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
5. Add the remaining ingredients (stewed tomatoes, bay leaves, Worcestershire sauce, pepper, salt, dried thyme, and cayenne pepper). Mix well.
6. Return the duck to the pot; bring to a boil.
7. Reduce heat and cover. Simmer for 60-75 minutes or until the duck is tender. Remove the duck.
8. Cool the duck, debone it, and cut into chunks. Return the chunks to the pan.
9. Simmer for an additional 5-10 minutes or until heated through.
10. Remove the bay leaves. Serve with hot cooked rice.
